title: ekebergia pterophylla
intro: Ekebergia pterophylla, also known as the Natal mahogany, is a deciduous tree native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Ekebergia pterophylla include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Ekebergia pterophylla typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or bark, which can be consumed as teas or used externally as poultices.
